title: Replace Template Haskell with DerivingVia/Generics entity deriving
description: Remove the mkEntity TH front-end (Manifest.Derive.TH, template-haskell dep) and replace it with a fully-derivable Entity via DerivingVia + Generics + DefaultSignatures. PrimKey becomes a standalone type family (computed from the record Rep, no per-entity line); rowDecoder/rowEncode/primKey get generic DefaultSignatures; a Table name carrier supplies the table name so plain entities are one 'deriving via (Table "users" UserT) instance Entity User' line, and cascade/RLS entities write a short explicit instance (table name + policy). Migrate all entities (fixtures, RLS Secret, typed-fields Account/Note, app schema). Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-deriving-via-entity-design.md.
close_reason: Replaced mkEntity TH with DerivingVia/Generics Entity; standalone PrimKey family, generic primKey, Table carrier; all entities migrated; 116/116; docs updated

title: Typed fields: newtype columns + typed primary/foreign keys
description: Make important fields distinct newtypes for type safety: typed primary keys and foreign keys (a UserId can't be confused with a PostId or filled from the wrong id) and general newtype-over-base column support (Email, Money). Hand-written newtypes made frictionless via deriving. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-typed-fields-newtypes-design.md. Slice A (typed-by-convention) approved; relationship-enforced FK<->PK matching (B) and mkEntity auto-gen are follow-ups.
close_reason: Slice A shipped: newtype columns (deriving ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta), typed primary keys, typed-by-convention foreign keys, demo round-trip + compile-failure golden + manual section. Relationship-enforced FK<->PK matching (B) and mkEntity id auto-gen remain as follow-ups.

title: Add support for PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS)
description:
Let Manifest work with PostgreSQL Row-Level Security so multi-tenant / per-user access policies are enforced by the database rather than hand-written WHERE clauses.

Scope to design:
- Session/transaction context: set RLS-relevant settings within a session or transaction so policies apply for that unit of work (e.g. SET LOCAL role = ..., or SET LOCAL <app.guc> = '<tenant>' at the start of withTransaction/withSession). Must be LOCAL-scoped so a pooled connection does not leak context to the next checkout.
- A typed API to declare/set the RLS context (e.g. withRlsContext / a SessionConfig hook) rather than raw SQL.
- Migrations: emit RLS DDL (ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, CREATE POLICY ...) from a declarative description, and diff it like columns.
- Connection-pool safety: ensure SET LOCAL / RESET semantics interact correctly with the STM pool in Manifest.Postgres (no context bleed across withConnection checkouts).

Open questions: GUC-variable vs SET ROLE approach; how policies are expressed (Haskell DSL vs raw SQL); interaction with the identity map (rows invisible under RLS must not be cached as 'absent').

## Context
Filed during SP4a (TH front-end). Follow-up feature, not part of SP4. Touches Manifest.Session (withTransaction/withConnection), the STM pool (Manifest.Postgres), and the migration engine (Manifest.Migrate).
acceptance_criteria: A documented way to scope an RLS context (role or GUC) to a session/transaction guaranteed not to leak across pooled connections; migrations can enable RLS and create/diff policies; a test proves a policy hides rows from the wrong tenant within a Manifest session.
close_reason: RLS shipped: typed policy DSL, declarative migration, withRlsContext, e2e tenant-isolation test.

title: Explore event sourcing / FRP support
description:
Investigate whether/how Manifest could support event sourcing and/or functional reactive programming (FRP) styles, as an alternative or complement to the current snapshot-diff Unit of Work.

Areas to explore:
- Event sourcing: model writes as an append-only event log (events as entities), with derived read models / projections. How would this sit alongside the identity-map + snapshot-diff session? Could 'save' optionally emit domain events? Event store schema, replay, snapshots/compaction.
- Projections: rebuilding read-model tables from the event stream; incremental projection updates (could reuse the migration/SQL machinery). Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY or logical replication as a change feed.
- FRP / reactive reads: expose query results as time-varying values / streams that update when underlying rows change (e.g. driven by LISTEN/NOTIFY or a polling layer). Relationship to the query builder.
- Scope question: is this a separate library layer on top of the Core, or woven into the session? What is the minimal useful slice (e.g. an append-only event entity + a projection helper) vs a full CQRS/ES framework.

Deliverable of THIS task: a design note / brainstorm (not an implementation) weighing the options, the fit with the existing HKD Core + UoW + query builder, and a recommended MVP slice if worth pursuing.
notes: Design PROPOSAL written to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-event-sourcing-frp-design.md. NOT APPROVED: it captures the brainstorm direction (opt-in ES aggregates; jsonb/aeson events; minimal store; per-aggregate projections; LISTEN/NOTIFY change feed; decomposed into 4 slices, ES core first). Awaiting review/approval before any implementation.

title: RLS: missing-ok current_setting variant (currentSettingOr)
description: RLS policy predicates use one-arg current_setting('app.x'), which errors when the GUC is unset (fail-closed). Offer a missing-ok variant (e.g. currentSettingOr :: Text -> Text -> Expr Text rendering current_setting('app.x', true) with a COALESCE default) so a policy can degrade to 'no rows' instead of erroring when context is absent. Follow-up to manifest-yyb.
close_reason: Added currentSettingOr (missing-ok current_setting with coalesce default); policy degrades to no-rows instead of erroring when context unset.
